decision: state_path is config-driven, default ./.osspulse/state.json
reasoning: Q1 clarification; matches config-file/env setup-once principle; default enables zero-config run
rejected: hardcoded ~/.osspulse/state.json
require explicit path, no default

decision: Each seen record stores identity key + first_seen_at (UTC ISO)
reasoning: Q2 clarification; identity satisfies V1 idempotency, timestamp is cheap now and is exactly what V2 delta-by-time needs, avoiding a state-format migration
rejected: identity keys only (set per repo)
full RawItem snapshot per item

decision: Keep StateStore Protocol (load/save) unchanged; add is_seen/mark_seen on the concrete adapter only
reasoning: Q3 clarification; respects github-collector-2 ADR-001 precedent (port signature untouched), keeps the dict as serialization contract, gives pipeline ergonomic dedup; promoting helpers to the Protocol is an S3 architect call
rejected: add helpers to the shared Protocol (larger blast radius)
pure load/save, dedup inline in pipeline (key-building logic leaks)

decision: Atomic write-temp-then-os.replace so crash mid-write cannot corrupt state
reasoning: architecture.md Transaction & Consistency; RF-1 mitigation
rejected: in-place truncate-and-write

decision: Fail loud (StateError) on malformed JSON; never silently reset to empty
reasoning: silent reset = loss of idempotency history (RF-2); zero-byte/missing-seen-key still tolerated as empty
rejected: silently reset corrupt file to empty state

decision: V1 only records seen state (write side); full delta-filtering is V2
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §5/§6 (V1 ghi, V2 dùng cho delta) — inferred scope boundary
rejected: implement full delta filtering in V1

decision: Single-operator single-process; no cross-process file locking in V1 (last-write-wins)
reasoning: project.md personal/self-host single operator; locking is out of scope
rejected: add file locking / advisory locks in V1

decision: State JSON version is integer 1 in V1; shape {"version":1,"seen":{"<repo>":{"<item_type>:<item_id>":"<first_seen_at>"}}}
reasoning: S2 testability — QA cannot assert 'correct version' without a concrete value; lock V1=1 and the document shape
rejected: leave version unspecified
use a string/semver version

decision: first_seen_at format = UTC ISO-8601 with trailing Z, consistent with RawItem.created_at
reasoning: S2 testability + consistency with collector output (e.g. 2026-06-20T08:15:30Z); avoids tz-ambiguous timestamps
rejected: naive local time
offset form +00:00 without Z

decision: Keep is_seen/mark_seen on concrete JsonFileStateStore; StateStore Protocol stays load/save unchanged
reasoning: AC-3-018 forbids altering the Protocol in V1; github-collector-2 ADR-001 precedent (port signature untouched); no second store exists yet so promoting is speculative
rejected: add is_seen/mark_seen to the StateStore Protocol

decision: Atomic write: tempfile in state_path.parent + flush + os.fsync + os.replace
reasoning: os.replace atomic only on same filesystem; temp in parent dir guarantees same volume; satisfies AC-3-007/008 (RF-1)
rejected: tempfile.mkstemp in system temp + shutil.move (cross-fs = copy, non-atomic)
in-place truncate-and-write

decision: StateError defined in src/osspulse/state/errors.py
reasoning: mirrors ConfigError per-module-error precedent; keeps state package self-contained and error reusable across future state adapters; models.py stays pure data
rejected: StateError in models.py
StateError inside json_store.py

decision: No openapi.yaml for this CLI-only change (R5 waiver)
reasoning: no inbound HTTP API; internal contract is the StateStore Protocol + JSON doc shape; cites R5 + cross-spec precedent ADR-007 (github-collector-2)
rejected: emit a contentless stub openapi.yaml

decision: JsonFileStateStore implemented with lazy-load cache, _identity_key helper, _now_utc_z, load/save/is_seen/mark_seen; StateStore Protocol left unchanged
reasoning: Follows design §Implementation Guide exactly; all 18 ACs satisfied; 38 tests written with AC-ID tags; coverage 100% on json_store.py
rejected: eager load on __init__ (design calls for lazy)
adding helpers to StateStore Protocol (AC-3-018 forbids)

decision: state_path field added to Config frozen dataclass with default './.osspulse/state.json'; load_config reads optional [state].state_path from TOML
reasoning: AC-3-013; default preserves both existing Config(...) call-sites (config.py:106, tests/test_models.py:23) — verified keyword construction, frozen dataclass, no call-site impact
rejected: hardcode path in adapter
require explicit path with no default

decision: Removed inner try/except Exception: raise block from save() — was a transparent re-raise adding dead branches
reasoning: The inner try/except Exception: raise was structurally a no-op (outer except OSError catches all I/O errors). It prevented 100% branch coverage on json_store.py and added no behaviour. Removing it is a minor simplification fully within design intent (ADR-002). No AC or error-mapping behaviour changes — StateError is still raised and temp file is still cleaned up.
rejected: keep the dead try/except and accept partial coverage
add a pragma: no cover comment

decision: load() does not guard isinstance(data, dict) after json.loads(); non-dict root JSON (null, [], 42) raises AttributeError instead of StateError
reasoning: json.loads() of 'null' returns Python None; None.get('version') raises AttributeError. The spec requires StateError for all structurally corrupt state. The load() Sequence Flow 1 checks JSONDecodeError (step 3) and version type (step 4) but has no step between them to verify the parsed value is a dict. Reproduced by QA probe for null, [], and 42.
fix: Add `if not isinstance(data, dict): raise StateError(f'state file is corrupt: expected a JSON object, got {type(data).__name__}')` in load() between JSONDecodeError catch and version check (after line 87 in json_store.py). Add regression tests test_null_root_raises_state_error and test_array_root_raises_state_error.
rejected: accept AttributeError as equivalent to StateError (violates spec's explicit error contract and caller expectations)

decision: Added isinstance(data, dict) guard in load() between JSONDecodeError catch and version check; removed dead outer branch in mark_seen(); deleted QA probe tests/qa_probe_nondicts.py
reasoning: RCA: load() called data.get('version') without first verifying data is a dict. json.loads('null') returns None; None.get() raises AttributeError. Fix inserts a guard at step 3b: if not isinstance(data, dict): raise StateError('state file is corrupt: expected a JSON object, got {type(data).__name__}'). Five regression tests added (null, [], 42, string roots — raise StateError + file-not-overwritten). Dead outer branch 'if changed or not items' in mark_seen() simplified to 'if changed' — identical runtime behavior, AC-3-006 verified unchanged. Full suite 119/119 pass; json_store.py 100% lines/branches; overall 98.94% lines; ruff clean.
rejected: accept AttributeError as handled (violates spec error contract)
silent reset on non-dict root (violates RF-2 no-reset invariant)

decision: GO — BUG-001 confirmed fixed and verified. AC-3-009 now fully covered. 119/119 tests pass; json_store.py 100% lines and branches; overall 98.94% lines / 98.21% branches; ruff clean. 18/18 ACs verified. QA probe deleted. No new bugs introduced. Gate clear for S6.
reasoning: isinstance(data, dict) guard at json_store.py lines 89-93 correctly raises StateError for null/[]/42/string root values. Five regression tests all pass independently (QA run: 119/119). mark_seen() dead-branch cleanup (OI-3) verified no behavior change — AC-3-006 tests pass. Test count matches developer S4-fix report exactly (119/119). Pre-existing infra gaps (.env.example, README, structured logging) are out of scope per orchestrator ruling.

decision: Change state-store-3 shipped and archived. Living spec written to openspec/specs/state-store/spec.md. Change moved to openspec/changes/archive/2026-06-24-state-store-3/.
reasoning: S5 GO received (18/18 ACs, 119/119 tests, 98.94% lines / 98.21% branches, ruff clean, BUG-001 fixed). openspec validate PASS before archive. openspec archive --yes executed successfully — 7 requirements merged into new living spec. Active changes list now empty (no active changes). release.md written with AC table, migration checklist (no DB migrations), rollback plan (git revert), post-deploy smoke tests, and deploy strategy. No breaking changes to Config (state_path has default). openapi.yaml waived (ADR-004, CLI-only change).
